entry.title: What is new in S/4HANA Manufacturing for Production Engineering & Operations (PEO) in 2025 FPS1
entry.content:
With the 2025 FPS1 release of SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for Production Engineering and Operations (PEO), SAP continues to strengthen its manufacturing capabilities by delivering features that boost engineering efficiency, improve shop floor flexibility, and streamline daily operations.

In this blog, I’ll walk you through the key innovations included in this feature pack – and what they mean for production engineers, supervisors, and manufacturing operations teams.

 

Production Engineering Efficiency Enhancements

🔁Alternative Sequences of Operations in Shop Floor Routings

PEO now allows production engineers to create alternative and parallel sequences directly within shop floor routings.

This is especially helpful when:

  • Work can be done on an alternative machine
  • Different inspection paths exist
  • Certain steps can be bypassed
  • Different lot-size specific process flows are required

Supervisors can switch to these sequences at the order level, giving operations more flexibility without modifying the standard routing.

 

Why it matters:

Greater process and scheduling flexibility → reduced lead time, better resource utilization, and faster response to shop floor contingencies. 

🧩 Mass Management of Components in Order-Specific Shop Floor Routings

Engineers can now manage multiple components at once—adding, editing, or replacing them in a single table-based view.

Items marked Not Editable remain protected.

Why it matters:

Huge efficiency gains during order changes and rework scenarios, where batch updates to components are common. 

🔄Manage Alternative Components Using Alternative Item Groups (AIGs)

PEO now supports creating and managing alternative item groups for order-specific shop floor routings.

You can:

  • Add, edit, or remove alternative components
  • Move components into new/existing AIGs
  • Create new AIGs for new components

Why it matters:

Allows the shop floor to use valid alternatives without touching master data — a big win for flexibility and compliance. 

🔧Flexible Rework Routings

Engineers can now create rework routings with flexible processing, enabling:

  • Sequence-independent processing
  • Cross-operation predecessor relationships

This requires the production order’s routing to have flexible processing enabled as well.

Why it matters:

Rework becomes smoother and more adaptable, reducing bottlenecks and manual coordination. 

📝  Long Texts for Externally Processed Operations

Long text descriptions can now be authored externally processed operations — and the system automatically transfers this text to:

  • The production order
  • The purchase requisition

Why it matters:

Clearer communication with suppliers + fewer manual updates = reduced errors and faster processing. 

🎨Custom Text Formats via New BAdI

A new BAdI allows companies to define custom heading formats in the rich text editor used for standard texts and work instructions.

Why it matters:

Guarantees consistent formatting aligned with corporate branding standards, without manual adjustments by engineers. 

2. Flexible Production Operations Enhancements

🔀Order Split for Serialized Products with Sequences

PEO now supports splitting shop floor orders even when serialized products use alternative or parallel sequences.

PEO automatically determines which sequences remain feasible for the new order.

Why it matters:

Significant time savings by allowing completed units to progress independently, reducing waiting time and simplifying sequencing.

 

⏸️ Action Handlers for Hold/Release Operations

Production process specialists can now configure automated or manual actions that trigger when applying or releasing a hold—for example:

  • Additional quality checks
  • Automatic follow-on system actions

 

Why it matters:

More controlled, automated, and auditable hold handling with reduced manual overhead.

 

🛠️ Additional Code Groups for Defect Recording

Supervisors can now define additional reason code groups for defect recording. Operators can then choose from any of these groups.

 

Why it matters:

More accurate defect categorization → richer insights and more effective quality improvement initiatives.

 

Wrapping Up

The 2025 FPS1 updates for SAP S/4HANA PEO focus on one overarching theme:

Empowering production engineers and shop floor teams with flexibility, speed, and clarity.

Whether it’s expanding routing capabilities, streamlining rework, improving communication with suppliers, or making shop floor execution more adaptable, these features help manufacturers operate with greater agility and precision.

If you’re already using PEO, many of these enhancements can immediately simplify daily operations. If you’re planning a transition to S/4HANA Manufacturing, these innovations highlight why PEO continues to be a strong foundation for digital manufacturing.

entry.title: Message no. CK869 display immediately / Meldungsnr. CK869 sofort anzeigen
entry.content:
Message no. CK 869 (Material XXXXXX in plant XXXX, itemised does not match cost component split) only appears when a calculation (CK13N) is recalled. This means that incorrect values can be posted in the material price without anyone noticing. Is there a solution to prevent this?

Kind regards

 

Die Meldungsnr. CK 869 (Für Material XXXXXX im Werk XXXX weicht Einzelnachweis von Kostenschichtung ab) erscheint erst bei Wiederaufruf einer Kalkulation (CK13N) . Dadurch können falsche Werte im Materialpreis gebucht werden, ohne dass es auffällt. Gibt es eine Lösung, um dies zu verhindern

Viele Grüße

entry.title: Re: Accounting Principles, Ledgers, and Parallel Accounting
entry.content:
Hi Venkathesh,

I hope that you got support at the time your question came up.

Just in case similar questions arise:  Industry-specific fiscal year variants, such as the 4-4-5 variant, are not supported as alternative fiscal year variants.
You can find more information, for example, in the following SAP Community blog post: In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA – Ledger Scoping Q&A.

Verena

entry.title: How to preview Excel file in SAPUI5 like PDFViewer?
entry.content:
I am working on an SAPUI5 / Fiori application.
For PDF files, I receive a Base64 string from OData response and preview it using sam.m.PDFViewer.
Now I want to do the same for an Excel (.xlsx) file.

Current Scenario

OData service returns Excel file

I can download the file successfully

But I want to preview Excel file inside my UI5 application.

Similar to how sam.m.PDFViewer  previews PDF.
Preview Excel Without forcing direct download.

entry.title: Re: Event based Revenue Recognition for service dokuments...
entry.content:
Hello Manuel,

Technically it should work like this:

In customizing activity Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition

(view cluster FINS_TRR) you should check for the applied assignment rule for the revenue keys under consideration. Example:

 

 

Then you can check under sources which source the revenue account 40000009 is assigned to.

In the step Assign Sources and Posting Rules for this source set the value for the cost element to 40000009. Revenue adjustment postings by EBRR will then also be posted to P&L account 40000009. Example:

 

 

 

Look up some further explanations on EBRR customizing in https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/f63dd39a28bb4b90adbf9e608aff58ea/917c9fa4c78e49c7853261070570337e.html?version=2023.latest

Such a change should be made with care and thoroughly tested. For instance, the revenue account and the revenue adjustment account usually have different semantic tags assigned in the financial statement version. These tags are used as far as I know in several reporting and analysis tools, for instance in the EBRR Fiori app Event-Based Revenue Recognition – Service Documents. If billed revenue and revenue adjustment account are the same, the same semantic tag will be applied for both which can potentially have a bunch of expected consequences. At least I have not heard of anyone trying this.

Best Regards

Frank

entry.title: Re: CDs view for the HSN number
entry.content:
Kindly check this blog to identify the released CDS view.

In your case, I_PRODUCTPLANTBASIC for Control Code (HSN) can be used. 

The CDS view PRODUCTPLANTBASIC has a field ConsumptionTaxCtrlCode ( https://api.sap.com/cdsviews/I_PRODUCTPLANTBASIC )

entry.title: Credit Memo Request to correct Debit Memo
entry.content:
We are using Debit Memo Request DR with subsequent Debit Memo L2. We now want to raise a credit note against the L2 Debit Memo/Invoice issues to customer. But there is no Credit Memo Request available, which allows to reference the L2 Document. 

How to resolve this problem?

Best Regards 

Johannes

entry.title: Customs VAT Supplier Invoice
entry.content:
Hi, 

Our customer is starting up business in BE importing products from outside of Europe. They will therefore receive invoices from customs including both DUTY and VAT but where the DUTY will be added to a PO and included in the inventory value.

What is the standard process for handling the VAT from Customs in Accounts Payable? I mean since it's based on the value that has been imported but placed on a separate invoice? I've seen different ways of handling it, but most of them seems to be creative-accounting. I'm therefore interested of understanding how the standard behavior should be since the gross amount and the VAT-amount will be the same?

Kind Regards, Sanne

entry.title: Re: not able to find the documents to download for tax cl...
entry.content:
Good day @jerrysam 

Thank you for your question.

The Doc No 2000004751 shown in your Excel is most likely the tax return document identifier, that was created potentially by the India Advance Return App, which is not a normal FI document number. That is why you cannot find it Display Journal Entry App.

You can view it by opening the same app and open monitor view and then search for the Doc No.

If it also created an accounting document, you could find it in Manage Journal Entries by searching in the Reference field with that Doc Number.

Below is a test on a European (not Indian specific) Advance Return app

Here I extract a list with a defined timeframe.

When I search in the system with the number 1800000000, I find it.

When I export the list, I can find the same number(s) without any other number. 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris
